heading: Basic energy sciences program
label: 18911 Basic energy sciences program
notes: CodificationSection is comprised of . Subsecs. (a), (b), and (c) of amended sections 18641, 16313, and 16315 of this title, respectively.section 10102 of div. B of Pub. L. 117–167section 10102 of div. B of Pub. L. 117–167
amendments: CodificationSection is comprised of . Subsecs. (a), (b), and (c) of amended sections 18641, 16313, and 16315 of this title, respectively.section 10102 of div. B of Pub. L. 117–167section 10102 of div. B of Pub. L. 117–167

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Director of the Office of Science shall support a program of research and development to bridge scientific barriers to, and expand theoretical and fundamental knowledge relevant to, understanding nuclear materials and matter for the benefit of commerce, medicine, and national security.

children.label: I
children.text: the advanced characterization of materials;

children.label: II
children.text: materials synthesis;

children.label: III
children.text: processing;

children.label: IV
children.text: the innovative use of experimental and theoretical data; and

children.label: V
children.text: mechanical behavior in unique environments, including the effects of radiation;

children.label: ii
children.text: electrochemistry research and associated techniques for processing nuclear materials;

children.label: iii
children.text: the development of advanced instrumentation and nuclear data collection to inform the activities described in clauses (i) and (ii); and

children.label: iv
children.text: any other area of research, as determined by the Director of the Office of Science; and

children.label: B
children.text: the Assistant Secretary for Nuclear Energy shall consult with the Director of the Office of Science to support the direction of translational research, development, and validation of physical concepts developed under the program.

children.heading: Funding
children.label: 3 Funding
children.text: Of the funds authorized to be appropriated for basic energy sciences in a fiscal year, there is authorized to be appropriated to the Secretary of Energy to carry out activities under this subsection $50,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2023 through 2027.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Director of the Office of Science (referred to in this subsection as the “Director”) shall establish a research initiative, to be known as the “Carbon Materials Science Initiative” (referred to in this subsection as the “Initiative”), to expand the fundamental knowledge of coal, coal-wastes, and carbon ore chemistry useful for understanding the conversion of carbon to material products.

children.heading: Coordination
children.label: B Coordination
children.text: In carrying out programs and activities under the Initiative, the Director shall leverage expertise and resources from the Office of Fossil Energy and Carbon Management and the United States Geological Survey.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: In carrying out the Initiative, the Director shall establish and organize activities among multidisciplinary teams to leverage, to the maximum extent practicable, expertise from the National Laboratories, institutions of higher education, and the private sector.

children.heading: Goals
children.label: ii Goals
children.text: The multidisciplinary teams described in clause (i) shall pursue expedient, milestone-driven research goals established by the Director.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Director shall carry out under the Initiative a program to support, and discover fundamental knowledge relevant to, carbon materials and carbon ore processing research.

children.label: i
children.text: methods of extraction, processing, recycling, and utilization of the materials and valuable minerals contained in raw coal and coal-waste;

children.label: ii
children.text: methods of improving performance, cost, and availability of materials for use in carbon capture systems; and

children.label: iii
children.text: unconventional pathways and materials for conversion of carbon dioxide molecules, minerals, and materials.

children.heading: Review
children.label: C Review
children.text: The Director shall periodically review activities carried out under the program described in subparagraph (A) to evaluate the achievement of scientific objectives and research milestones.

children.heading: Coordination with existing programs and centers
children.label: D Coordination with existing programs and centers
I
children.text: the United States Geological Survey; and

children.label: II
children.text: the programs and the Carbon Utilization Research Center established under ; andsection 16298a of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: avoid duplication of efforts to the maximum extent practicable.

children.label: i
children.text: be known as a “Carbon Materials Research Center” (referred to in this paragraph as a “Center”); and

children.label: I
children.text: developing and advancing methods of extracting, processing, or recycling carbon or other valuable materials or minerals from raw coal, coal-waste, or other solid carbon materials, for the development of new carbon-based materials;

children.label: II
children.text: methods of improving the structural, physical, and chemical properties of carbon-based materials or other valuable materials from raw coal, coal-waste, or other solid carbon materials and their recyclability;

children.label: III
children.text: overcoming the challenges and maximizing the benefits of commercially extracting, producing, or improving coal-derived carbon and resulting products; and

children.label: IV
children.text: identifying novel pathways and materials for carbon storage and conversion into useful products.

children.label: i
children.text: select Centers under subparagraph (A) on a competitive, merit-reviewed basis; and

children.label: ii
children.text: consider applications from the National Laboratories, institutions of higher education, multi-institutional collaborations, and other appropriate entities.

children.heading: Duration
children.label: C Duration
children.text: A Center shall receive support for a period of not more than 5 years beginning on the date of establishment of that Center, subject to the availability of appropriations.

children.heading: Renewal
children.label: D Renewal
children.text: On the expiration of any period of support of a Center, the Director may renew support for that Center, on a merit-reviewed basis, for a period of not more than 5 years.

children.label: i
children.text: ensure that the research activities carried out by the Centers are not duplicative of existing efforts; and

children.label: ii
children.text: if practicable, leverage existing user facilities and other capabilities of the Department of Energy to carry out the research objectives of the Centers.

children.heading: Carbon Sequestration Research and Geologic Computational Science Initiative
children.label: f Carbon Sequestration Research and Geologic Computational Science Initiative

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary of Energy (referred to in this subsection as the “Secretary”) shall establish a research initiative, to be known as the “Carbon Sequestration Research and Geologic Computational Science Initiative” (referred to in this subsection as the “Initiative”), to expand the fundamental knowledge, data collection, data analysis, and modeling of subsurface geology for the purpose of advancing carbon sequestration in geologic formations.

children.heading: Leveraging
children.label: B Leveraging
children.text: In carrying out programs and activities under the Initiative, the Secretary shall leverage expertise and resources from the Office of Fossil Energy and Carbon Management and the United States Geological Survey.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: In carrying out the Initiative, the Secretary shall establish and organize activities among multidisciplinary teams to leverage, to the maximum extent practicable, expertise from the National Laboratories, institutions of higher education, and the private sector.

children.heading: Goals
children.label: ii Goals
children.text: The multidisciplinary teams described in clause (i) shall pursue aggressive, milestone-driven research goals established by the Secretary.

children.heading: Additional activities
children.label: D Additional activities
children.text: The Secretary may organize additional activities under this subsection through other organizational structures.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary shall carry out under the Initiative a program to support research needed for, and discover knowledge relevant to, the sequestration of carbon in geologic formations.

children.label: i
children.text: gathering geologic data for pore space characterization, including improvements to geologic seismic imaging;

children.label: ii
children.text: evaluating pore space quality, including evaluation of geologic samples, to determine appropriate sequestration zones for carbon;

children.label: iii
children.text: testing carbon sequestration;

children.label: iv
children.text: monitoring carbon migration in geologic formations;

children.label: v
children.text: advancements in data analytics, including the analysis of seismic data, and computational science to improve the advanced computing, visualization, and imaging of geologic formations for the sequestration of carbon; and

children.label: vi
children.text: predictive understanding of coupled processes in complex subsurface geologic systems for secure carbon storage.

children.heading: Review
children.label: C Review
children.text: The Secretary shall periodically review activities carried out under the program described in subparagraph (A) to evaluate achievement of scientific objectives and research milestones.

children.heading: Carbon storage research and geologic computational science centers
children.label: 3 Carbon storage research and geologic computational science centers

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: In carrying out the activities authorized under paragraph (2), the Secretary shall select and establish not more than 2 carbon storage research and geologic computational science centers (referred to in this paragraph as a “Center”) to develop and advance improvements to data collection, analysis, and modeling of subsurface geology for the purpose of advancing carbon sequestration in geologic formations.

children.label: I
children.text: select Centers under subparagraph (A) on a competitive, merit-reviewed basis; and

children.label: II
children.text: to the maximum extent practicable, locate each Center in a geographically diverse region with established and ongoing geologic carbon sequestration research and demonstration.

children.heading: Applications
children.label: ii Applications
children.text: In selecting Centers under subparagraph (A), the Secretary shall consider applications from institutions of higher education, multi-institutional collaborations, and other appropriate entities.

children.heading: New Centers
children.label: i New Centers
children.text: A Center established after , shall receive support for a period of not more than 5 years beginning on the date of establishment of that Center, subject to the availability of appropriations.August 9, 2022

children.heading: Existing Centers
children.label: ii Existing Centers
children.text: A Center already in existence on , may continue to receive support for a period of not more than 5 years beginning on .August 9, 2022August 9, 2022

children.heading: Renewal
children.label: iii Renewal
children.text: On expiration of a period of support described in clause (i) or (ii), the Secretary may renew support for the Center, on a merit-reviewed basis, for a period of not more than 5 years.

children.heading: Coordination with existing programs and Centers
children.label: 4 Coordination with existing programs and Centers
i
children.text: the United States Geological Survey; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the programs established under ; andsection 16293 of this title

children.label: B
children.text: avoid duplication of efforts to the maximum extent practicable.

children.heading: Funding for Carbon Initiatives
children.label: g Funding for Carbon Initiatives
children.text: Of the funds authorized to be appropriated for basic energy sciences in a fiscal year, there is authorized to be appropriated to the Secretary to carry out activities under subsections (e) and (f) $50,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2023 through 2027.
